message:
fix(ext,vtab): return NULL/0 for cursor column/rowid past EOF instead of panicking

All virtual table cursors (FTS5, JSON each/tree, R-tree, generate_series,
dbstat, carray, and test cursors) now gracefully handle column() and rowid()
calls when the cursor is past EOF or when the column index is out of range.

Before: column() on an exhausted cursor would panic or return an error,
which propagated as a confusing FrankenError to the SQL layer. rowid()
returned stale values.

After: column() returns SqliteValue::Null and rowid() returns 0 when
the cursor is past EOF, matching SQLite's behavior where reading past
the end of a result set produces NULL values.

Specific changes:
- fsqlite-ext-fts5: fix rank column detection (col == -1 OR col == num_cols)
  and add NULL for out-of-range positive indices instead of falling into
  the rank branch. Added tests for OOB column indices.
- fsqlite-ext-json: json_each and json_tree cursors return NULL/0 instead
  of FrankenError when pos is past rows.len(). Consistent with SQLite's
  json_each behavior on exhausted cursors.
- fsqlite-ext-rtree: column() and rowid() guard on eof(). Added tests
  for exhausted R-tree cursors.
- fsqlite-ext-misc: generate_series, dbstat, carray cursors guard on eof().
- fsqlite-func/vtab.rs: test cursors (CounterCursor, AlwaysEmptyCursor,
  MultiColumnCursor) guard on eof().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
